body:
DAN cycle: full e2e audit of NatsTransportBridge. VERDICT: DO NOT START the bridge. Structural re-gating is correct, but I found one HIGH constitutional defect (N1) that is BROADER than federation, plus two HIGH federation-boundary gaps. Full record: _PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE_BASE/reports/CLAUDE_NATS_BRIDGE_AUDIT_2026-06-14.md.

PASS: inbound federated msgs ARE re-gated (._inbound -> from_wire validate/coerce -> MembraneBus.publish = Omega-8/freshness/trust chokepoint; no bypass). Wire round-trips canonically. Optional/offline/localhost/lazy-connect confirmed. Echo guard correct.

*** N1 - HIGH, FIX NOW regardless of federation (affects the LOCAL bus): Omega-8 misses sub-typed action sigma. is_action_layer() matches ONLY exact 'm.action' (+cm.* set +'cmd.' prefix). VERIFIED: m.action.helm, m.action.rudder.set, m.actuation, m.command all return False -> NOT subject to Omega-8. PoC via offline wire helpers: an 'm.action.helm' envelope was DELIVERED through MembraneBus.publish in BOTH WAKE and DREAM (Omega-8 never fired). This silently defeats 'no action in DREAM' the moment anyone uses the natural sub-typed naming (m.action.helm IS what a helm command looks like). Latent today (no sub-typed action sigma in live code, m.action.* unrouted, no Orbi executor) and UNTESTED (tests only assert is_action_layer==False for non-actions; none pins a sub-typed action as True). FIX: sigma=='m.action' or sigma.startswith('m.action.'); review m.actuation/m.command namespaces; add tests pinning sub-typed action as action-layer + Omega-8-suppressed in DREAM. Vendored file ai_chi/_vendor/mebus/protocol.py - patch with vendoring note. ***

N2 - HIGH (federation): trust gate defeated by self-asserted kappa.trust_score. effective_trust() reads the message's OWN trust_score (default 1.0); inbound is NOT attenuated. VERIFIED forged trust_score=1.0 passes floor, 0.0 blocked. With unauth NATS, any LAN actor injects 'fully trusted' cognition. FIX: attenuate/stamp inbound trust (per-node ceiling), re-stamp provenance, require NATS auth+TLS. Pre-start blocker.

N3 - HIGH (federation): subscribe('mebus.>') ingests the action layer. With N1+N2 a peer can inject action-class msgs; even N1-fixed, action in WAKE isn't Omega-8-suppressed and relies on the (forgeable) trust floor. FIX: don't subscribe to action subjects inbound, or drop inbound action-layer via explicit federation policy. Pre-executor blocker.

N4 - MED: no outbound sigma-allowlist; _federated_publish mirrors ALL local publishes onto the LAN (cognition/positions/action intents). FIX: outbound allowlist.
N5 - MED: no NATS auth/TLS; nats:// plaintext. Required pre non-loopback.
N6 - LOW: docstring says 'JetStream' but uses core NATS (best-effort, no durability). Fine per ADR-0001 (ledger=truth); fix docstring.
N7 - LOW: routing_key double-prefix 'mebus.m.m.action.helm'.
N8 - LOW: monkeypatch race swapping bus.publish from loop thread.

Bridge stays stop-gated/dormant (its .start() socket connect is the gated act). I did NOT edit code (Auditor-Scribe). verify_floor True.
constraints: do not start NatsTransportBridge until N2+N3+N5 closed
gates enforced at the membrane; federation is a trust boundary
Omega-8 must catch all action-layer sigma incl. sub-typed
forbidden_actions: start NatsTransportBridge / socket connect without owner approval
weaken Omega-8 / trust floor / membrane chokepoint
objective: NatsTransportBridge e2e safety audit + Omega-8 classifier defect
requested_output: PatchPlan: N1 fix is_action_layer to catch sub-typed action sigma (+tests) NOW; N2 attenuate inbound federation trust + provenance; N3 exclude/drop inbound action-layer; N4 outbound allowlist; N5 NATS auth+TLS; N6 docstring; keep bridge dormant
summary: NatsTransportBridge: inbound re-gating correct, but DO NOT START. N1 (HIGH, fix now, affects local bus): Omega-8's is_action_layer misses sub-typed action sigma (m.action.helm escapes suppression in DREAM - PoC verified, latent+untested). N2/N3 (HIGH federation): self-asserted trust forgeable + mebus.> ingests action layer. N4/N5 MED, N6-N8 LOW. All Codex-lane; bridge stays stop-gated.
